close_reason: Broadened 401 hint to 'check credentials and permissions'
description:
src/lib.rs:240-247 maps 401 to 'GCP_ACCESS_TOKEN may have expired; reconstruct the backend with a fresh token'. A 401 from GCP's secretmanager.googleapis.com can also indicate:
  - Token was malformed at construction (typo on copy-paste from gcloud auth print-access-token).
  - Token was revoked (admin action on the user/service account).
  - Token's parent identity was disabled.
The 'may have expired' wording suggests the user only needs to re-run gcloud auth print-access-token. For the malformed/revoked cases this misleads — re-running the same action with the same broken state will not help.

Suggested rewrite: 'authentication failed (HTTP 401); regenerate GCP_ACCESS_TOKEN with <REDACTED_GCP_TOKEN>; if the new token also fails, verify the identity is enabled and has roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor on the project'.

Trade-off: longer message, more accurate. Engineering basis: the user reading the error at 2am does not know GCP IAM internals; the error should point at the most likely first-line fixes.
title: 401 hint message focused only on token expiry; misleads on other auth failures
